from target import wildcard_match

def test_wildcard_match():
    # Simple exact match
    assert wildcard_match("hello", "hello") is True
    # Single-character wildcard '?'
    assert wildcard_match("h?llo", "hello") is True
    # Multi-character wildcard '*', including empty match
    assert wildcard_match("he*o", "heo") is True
    # Combination of '*' and '?' with mismatch
    assert wildcard_match("a*?b", "ab") is False
    # Edge case: pattern only '*', should match any string including empty
    assert wildcard_match("*", "") is True
    assert wildcard_match("*", "anything") is True
    # Edge case: empty pattern matches only empty string
    assert wildcard_match("", "") is True
    assert wildcard_match("", "nonempty") is False

from target import wildcard_match

def test_wildcard_match():
    # Simple exact match
    assert wildcard_match("hello", "hello") is True
    # Single-character wildcard '?'
    assert wildcard_match("h?llo", "hello") is True
    # '*' matches any sequence including empty
    assert wildcard_match("he*o", "heo") is True
    # '*' matches multiple characters
    assert wildcard_match("a*bc", "axyzbc") is True
    # Combination of '?' and '*'
    assert wildcard_match("a?*c", "abXYZc") is True
    # Edge case: empty pattern matches empty string
    assert wildcard_match("", "") is True
    # Edge case: pattern with only '*' matches empty string
    assert wildcard_match("*", "") is True
    # Non-matching cases
    assert wildcard_match("abc", "ab") is False
    assert wildcard_match("a*c?d", "abcd") is False
    # Pattern longer than string without '*'
    assert wildcard_match("abcd", "abc") is False
